display.purpose: Page titles and main workout names.

headline.purpose: Card titles and major secondary headings.

label.purpose: Metadata chips, filters, and compact status text.

shadows.name: shell-glow
shadows.purpose: Outer app frame and hero shell.

shadows.name: badge-lift
shadows.purpose: Floating icon badges and compact circular controls.

motion.name: ease-hover
motion.purpose: Default easing for hover lift and brightness transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single most important action in a panel, used for opening or resuming a workout.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A supportive secondary action for back-navigation, setup, and lower-pressure tasks.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;min-height:48px;padding:12px 16px;border:1px solid #e2e8f0;border-radius:9999px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#ffffff,#eff6ff);color:#2563eb;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:14px;font-weight:600;line-height:1;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.08);transition:transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1),background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1),border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1);cursor:pointer}.ds-btn-secondary:hover{transform:translateY(-2px);background:#ffffff;border-color:#cbd5e1}.ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible{outline:2px solid #6ee7f9;outline-offset:3px}.ds-btn-secondary:active{transform:translateY(0)}

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Compact metadata chip for workout state, auth mode, and progress cues.

components.name: Search Input
components.description: The lightweight search field used on history and utility screens.
components.html: <label class="ds-input-search"><svg class="ds-input-icon" viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true"><circle cx="11" cy="11" r="7" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8"></circle><path d="M20 20L16.65 16.65" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-linecap="round"></path></svg><input type="search" placeholder="Search by workout name" /></label>

components.name: Elevated Card
components.description: The default container for summaries, history rows, and workout preview blocks.
components.html:
Today

Upper Body Session

Start the day with a clear plan and keep every set easy to log.
components.css: .ds-card{padding:20px;border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.8);border-radius:28px;background:rgba(255,255,255,0.85);box-shadow:0 16px 40px rgba(148,163,184,0.12);backdrop-filter:blur(12px);color:#0b1330}.ds-card-kicker{margin:0;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:14px;font-weight:500;line-height:1.5;color:#2563eb}.ds-card-title{margin:12px 0 0;font-family:"Avenir Next Condensed","Trebuchet MS","Avenir Next",sans-serif;font-size:30px;font-weight:600;line-height:1.1;letter-spacing:-0.025em}.ds-card-body{margin:8px 0 0;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:14px;line-height:1.5;color:#55607a}

components.name: Filter Pill
components.description: The in-page filter control used on history and archive screens.
components.html: CompletedPartial
components.css: .ds-filter-row{display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:8px}.ds-filter-pill{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:8px 16px;border:1px solid #e2e8f0;border-radius:9999px;background:#ffffff;color:#55607a;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:14px;font-weight:600;line-height:1;text-decoration:none;transition:border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1),color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1),transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1)}.ds-filter-pill:hover{border-color:#cbd5e1;color:#0b1330;transform:translateY(-1px)}.ds-filter-pill:focus-visible{outline:2px solid #6ee7f9;outline-offset:3px}.ds-filter-pill.is-active{background:#0b1330;border-color:#0b1330;color:#ffffff}

components.name: Exercise Media Tile
components.description: The signature exercise tile that gives quick identity without tipping into gamification.
components.html:
Push

Incline Press
components.css: .ds-exercise-tile{position:relative;overflow:hidden;min-height:96px;padding:8px;border:1px solid rgba(191,219,254,0.8);border-radius:22px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#eff6ff,#dbeafe 55%,#f5d0fe);box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.06)}.ds-exercise-tile::before{content:"";position:absolute;left:-12px;top:10px;width:40px;height:40px;border-radius:9999px;background:rgba(125,211,252,0.55);filter:blur(20px)}.ds-exercise-tile::after{content:"";position:absolute;right:-8px;bottom:10px;width:48px;height:48px;border-radius:9999px;background:rgba(244,114,182,0.4);filter:blur(22px)}.ds-exercise-chip{position:relative;display:inline-flex;padding:4px 8px;border:1px solid #bfdbfe;border-radius:9999px;background:rgba(255,255,255,0.8);color:#2563eb;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:10px;font-weight:600;line-height:1.2;letter-spacing:0.18em;text-transform:uppercase;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.06)}.ds-exercise-name{position:relative;margin:28px 28px 0 0;color:#082f49;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:12px;font-weight:600;line-height:1.35}.ds-exercise-badge{position:absolute;right:10px;bottom:10px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:28px;height:28px;border-radius:9999px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#2563eb,#0ea5e9);color:#ffffff;box-shadow:0 10px 24px rgba(15,23,42,0.18)}.ds-exercise-badge svg{width:14px;height:14px}

components.name: App Shell
components.description: The outer frame that makes the app feel protected, soft, and mobile-close.
components.css: .ds-app-shell{padding:10px;border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.8);border-radius:32px;background:rgba(255,255,255,0.7);backdrop-filter:blur(20px);box-shadow:0 24px 70px rgba(15,23,42,0.12),0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.35),0 0 40px rgba(96,165,250,0.1)}.ds-app-shell-inner{overflow:hidden;border-radius:28px;background:radial-gradient(circle at 18% 18%, rgba(56,189,248,0.18), transparent 28%),radial-gradient(circle at 82% 20%, rgba(244,114,182,0.16), transparent 24%),radial-gradient(circle at 50% 100%, rgba(34,197,94,0.12), transparent 28%),linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.94) 0%, rgba(244,247,255,0.96) 100%)}.ds-app-shell-demo{padding:24px;font-family:"Avenir Next Condensed","Trebuchet MS","Avenir Next",sans-serif;font-size:30px;font-weight:600;line-height:1.1;letter-spacing:-0.025em;color:#0b1330}

components.name: Auth Chip
components.description: The small identity and mode marker used to show who is training and whether the app is live or mock-backed.
components.css: .ds-auth-row{display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:8px;align-items:center}.ds-auth-chip{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;max-width:100%;padding:4px 12px;border:1px solid #bfdbfe;border-radius:9999px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#f0f9ff,#dbeafe);color:#2563eb;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:12px;font-weight:600;line-height:1.2;letter-spacing:0.16em;text-transform:uppercase;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.06)}.ds-signout-chip{padding:8px 12px;border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.7);border-radius:9999px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#ffffff,#f8fafc);color:#475569;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:12px;font-weight:600;line-height:1;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.06);transition:transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1),background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1);cursor:pointer}.ds-signout-chip:hover{transform:translateY(-2px);background:#ffffff}.ds-signout-chip:focus-visible{outline:2px solid #6ee7f9;outline-offset:3px}

components.name: Metric Card
components.description: The compact stat block used for duration, notes, sets, and workout summary counts.

components.name: Rest Timer
components.description: The recovery component that turns timing into a gentle nudge instead of a loud countdown.
components.html:
Rest

01:30
components.css: .ds-rest-timer{display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:12px;padding:12px;border:1px solid #fde68a;border-radius:16px;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#fffdf4,#fff7ed);box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.06)}.ds-rest-label{margin:0;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:11px;font-weight:600;line-height:1.2;letter-spacing:0.18em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#b45309}.ds-rest-value{margin:6px 0 0;font-family:"Avenir Next","Segoe UI","Helvetica Neue",sans-serif;font-size:18px;font-weight:600;line-height:1.2;color:#0b1330}.ds-rest-actions{display:flex;gap:8px}.ds-rest-icon{display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;width:40px;height:40px;border:none;border-radius:9999px;background:#ffffff;color:#b45309;box-shadow:0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.06);transition:transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1),filter 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1);cursor:pointer}.ds-rest-icon:hover{transform:translateY(-2px);filter:brightness(1.03)}.ds-rest-icon:focus-visible{outline:2px solid #6ee7f9;outline-offset:3px}.ds-rest-icon svg{width:16px;height:16px}

components.name: Feedback Panel
components.description: The compact state message used for success, warnings, and action feedback.
components.html: Upper body day is back on the calendar for tomorrow.

narrative.overview:
LiftLog should feel like a training notebook opened in clean daylight, a page you trust, a pace you can keep. It is not trying to psych the user up or impress them with performance theater. It should meet a self-coached lifter at working height: phone in hand, between sets, attention split, needing calm structure more than stimulation.

The system gets its warmth from pale page tones, soft halos, and rounded containers that feel more like protective sleeves than glossy cards. Blue carries action, emerald and amber explain state, and violet appears as a small note of optimism rather than spectacle. The atmosphere should feel like quiet forward motion: fresh, breathable, and gently disciplined.

This system explicitly rejects anything overbearing, macho, performative, hyper-gamified, bloated, clinical, or cold. If a screen feels like a challenge app, a supplement ad, an enterprise dashboard, or a medical intake form, it is off-brand.
keyCharacteristics: Daylit surfaces with selective color energy.
Rounded, hand-friendly controls sized for live use during a workout.
Encouraging tone carried by clarity, not hype.
Ambient depth that keeps the UI soft, breathable, and trustworthy.

rules.name: The Encouragement-Not-Hype Rule
rules.body: Blue leads the product voice. Emerald and amber explain state, but they never take over a screen. If the palette starts feeling competitive, loud, or game-like, pull color back to Page Mist and Tint Wash.

rules.name: The Journal Cover Rule
rules.body: Display type appears to anchor a screen, not to sell it. If a heading sounds like a campaign slogan, the typography is being asked to do the wrong job.

rules.name: The Quiet Lift Rule
rules.body: Shadows must diffuse outward and disappear into the page. If the UI starts to look dense, metallic, or 2014-app glossy, the blur is too small and the opacity is too high.

dos: Do keep the primary action in a pill-shaped control (9999px) and reserve the blue-cyan-violet gradient for the single most important action in a pane.
Do use Page Mist (#f6f8ff), Tint Wash (#eff6ff), emerald washes, and amber washes to explain state before introducing stronger saturation.
Do keep labels short, uppercase only when they behave like metadata, and tracked around 0.16em to 0.18em.
Do let shadows stay ambient and diffuse. The interface should feel cushioned, not carved.
donts: Don't make it overbearing. No alarm-red default states, no challenge-language theatrics, and no interface patterns that pressure the user into intensity.
Don't use macho or performative gym cues. Black-metal palettes, grit textures, flame gradients, and bodybuilding-poster typography are prohibited.
Don't drift into hyper-gamified habit pressure or bloated dashboard energy with trophy counts, streak worship, or walls of metric cards.
Don't make it clinical or cold. If a screen feels like medical intake software or enterprise admin UI, the color warmth and rounded softness are wrong.
